implementations

Class MyStack<E>

  • java.lang.Object
    • implementations.MyStack<E>
  • Type Parameters:
    E - The type of elements held in this stack.
    All Implemented Interfaces:
    StackADT<E>

    public class MyStack<E>
extends java.lang.Object
implements StackADT<E>
    MyStack is an array-list-based implementation of the StackADT interface. It follows the Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) principle and uses MyArrayList as its underlying data structure.

    The top of the stack corresponds to the last index of the internal MyArrayList. This allows O(1) push and pop operations.

    This implementation does not use any classes from the java.util.* collection framework (except EmptyStackException and NoSuchElementException which are standard Java exceptions).

    Version:
    2.0
    Author:
    Vansh

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      MyStack()
      Constructs an empty MyStack.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void clear()
      Removes all elements from this stack.
      boolean contains(E toFind)
      Returns true if this stack contains the specified element.
      boolean equals(StackADT<E> that)
      Compares the specified StackADT with this stack for equality.
      boolean isEmpty()
      Returns true if this stack contains no elements.
      Iterator<E> iterator()
      Returns an iterator over the elements in this stack, from top to bottom.
      E peek()
      Returns the element at the top of this stack without removing it.
      E pop()
      Removes and returns the element at the top of this stack.
      void push(E toAdd)
      Pushes the specified element onto the top of this stack.
      int search(E toFind)
      Returns the 1-based position from the top of the stack where the specified element is located.
      int size()
      Returns the number of elements currently in this stack.
      boolean stackOverflow()
      Returns true if the stack is at capacity.
      java.lang.Object[] toArray()
      Returns an Object array containing all elements in this stack in order from top to bottom.
      E[] toArray(E[] toHold)
      Returns an array containing all elements in this stack in order from top to bottom.

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        equals, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

    • Constructor Detail

      • MyStack

        public MyStack()
        Constructs an empty MyStack.

    • Method Detail

      • push

        public void push(E toAdd)
          throws java.lang.NullPointerException
        Pushes the specified element onto the top of this stack.

        Pre-condition: toAdd must not be null.

        Post-condition: The element is placed on top of the stack and the stack size increases by one.

        Specified by:
        push in interface StackADT<E>
        Parameters:
        toAdd - The element to push onto the stack.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - If the specified element is null.

      • pop

        public E pop()
      throws java.util.EmptyStackException
        Removes and returns the element at the top of this stack.

        Pre-condition: The stack must not be empty.

        Post-condition: The top element is removed and the stack size decreases by one.

        Specified by:
        pop in interface StackADT<E>
        Returns:
        The element removed from the top of the stack.
        Throws:
        java.util.EmptyStackException - If the stack is empty.

      • peek

        public E peek()
       throws java.util.EmptyStackException
        Returns the element at the top of this stack without removing it.

        Pre-condition: The stack must not be empty.

        Post-condition: The stack remains unchanged.

        Specified by:
        peek in interface StackADT<E>
        Returns:
        The element at the top of the stack.
        Throws:
        java.util.EmptyStackException - If the stack is empty.

      • clear

        public void clear()
        Removes all elements from this stack.

        Pre-condition: None.

        Post-condition: The stack is empty and its size is zero.

        Specified by:
        clear in interface StackADT<E>

      • isEmpty

        public boolean isEmpty()
        Returns true if this stack contains no elements.
        Specified by:
        isEmpty in interface StackADT<E>
        Returns:
        true if the stack is empty; false otherwise.

      • toArray

        public java.lang.Object[] toArray()
        Returns an Object array containing all elements in this stack in order from top to bottom.

        Pre-condition: None.

        Post-condition: The stack remains unchanged.

        Specified by:
        toArray in interface StackADT<E>
        Returns:
        An array containing all elements of the stack from top to bottom.

      • toArray

        public E[] toArray(E[] toHold)
            throws java.lang.NullPointerException
        Returns an array containing all elements in this stack in order from top to bottom. If the given array is large enough it is used; otherwise a new array of the same runtime type is allocated.

        Pre-condition: The specified array must not be null.

        Post-condition: The stack remains unchanged.

        Specified by:
        toArray in interface StackADT<E>
        Parameters:
        toHold - The array into which the elements of this stack are to be stored, if it is big enough.
        Returns:
        An array containing all stack elements from top to bottom.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - If the specified array is null.

      • contains

        public boolean contains(E toFind)
                 throws java.lang.NullPointerException
        Returns true if this stack contains the specified element.

        Pre-condition: toFind must not be null.

        Post-condition: The stack remains unchanged.

        Specified by:
        contains in interface StackADT<E>
        Parameters:
        toFind - The element to search for.
        Returns:
        true if the element is found; false otherwise.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - If the specified element is null.

      • search

        public int search(E toFind)
        Returns the 1-based position from the top of the stack where the specified element is located. The topmost element is at position 1. Returns -1 if the element is not found.

        Pre-condition: None.

        Post-condition: The stack remains unchanged.

        Specified by:
        search in interface StackADT<E>
        Parameters:
        toFind - The element to search for.
        Returns:
        The 1-based position from the top, or -1 if not found.

      • iterator

        public Iterator<E> iterator()
        Returns an iterator over the elements in this stack, from top to bottom.

        Pre-condition: None.

        Post-condition: The stack remains unchanged.

        Specified by:
        iterator in interface StackADT<E>
        Returns:
        An Iterator over the stack elements from top to bottom.

      • equals

        public boolean equals(StackADT<E> that)
        Compares the specified StackADT with this stack for equality. Two stacks are equal if they have the same size and all corresponding elements are equal when compared top to bottom.

        Pre-condition: None.

        Post-condition: Both stacks remain unchanged.

        Specified by:
        equals in interface StackADT<E>
        Parameters:
        that - The stack to compare with this stack.
        Returns:
        true if both stacks contain the same elements in the same order; false otherwise.

      • size

        public int size()
        Returns the number of elements currently in this stack.
        Specified by:
        size in interface StackADT<E>
        Returns:
        The number of elements in the stack.

      • stackOverflow

        public boolean stackOverflow()
        Returns true if the stack is at capacity. Since this is a dynamically sized stack with no fixed upper bound, this method always returns false.
        Specified by:
        stackOverflow in interface StackADT<E>
        Returns:
        false always, as this stack has no fixed capacity.
